methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DroneForge is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $2.5M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of four private peers in the sector (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air), though no public comparables exist. The narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable method here. Using total capital raised ($2.5M) as a proxy for pre-seed valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $6M. This represents the primary quantitative anchor for the analysis, reflecting normalized early-stage capital efficiency within the autonomous systems category.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. This range reflects reasonable upside and downside variance around the stage-progression result, though the single-method basis introduces elevated uncertainty. Key risks to valuation include the absence of comparable same-stage transactions in the sector, making the 2.5x multiplier assumption material to the outcome. Future validation would require either comparable pre-seed or seed-stage transaction data from the autonomous systems space, or advancement to a measurable inflection (product traction, customer validation) that could support alternative methods.
